title: Delete templates/hooks/ directory (vendored-session.sh + pre-commit)
body:
Remove the entire `templates/hooks/` directory. This contains two files that are dead after the image-baked migration:

- `templates/hooks/vendored-session.sh` (90 lines) — session-time orchestrator that ran post-install health checks at SessionStart. The medici image now has its own health check in session.sh.
- `templates/hooks/pre-commit` (88 lines) — git pre-commit hook template that prevented editing protected vendor files.

Also remove the runtime copy at `.vendored/hooks/` if it exists in the repo tree (distinct from the templates).

## Acceptance criteria

1. `templates/hooks/` directory no longer exists
2. No runtime `.vendored/hooks/` directory in the repo tree
3. `git grep vendored-session.sh` returns no production code hits (commit history is fine)
4. `git grep pre-commit` in `templates/` returns nothing

title: Strip hook installation logic from install command
body:
Remove all hook-wiring code from the install command (`templates/install`) and the bootstrap installer (`install.sh`).

### In templates/install (~lines 1305-1490):
- `setup_hooks_claude()` (lines ~1314-1357) — writes .claude/settings.json with SessionStart hooks
- `setup_hooks_codex()` (lines ~1360-1393) — writes .codex/config.toml with session-start hooks
- `setup_hooks(agent)` (lines ~1396-1422) — dispatcher for both agents
- `list_hooks(hook_type)` (lines ~1455-1489) — lists vendor hook script paths
- `ORCHESTRATOR_PATH` constant (line ~1307)
- The `--setup-hooks` CLI flag and its argparse wiring

### In install.sh:
- Line ~50: creates `.vendored/hooks/` directory
- Lines ~92-95: downloads vendored-session.sh
- Line ~161: calls `python3 .vendored/install --setup-hooks`

### Keep:
- `run_post_install_hook()` (~line 658) — still used by core vendoring for per-vendor post-install scripts
- `list_vendors()` (~line 1432) — still used for host-side operations
- All core install logic (fetch, copy, manifest writing)

## Acceptance criteria

1. `--setup-hooks` flag removed from install command
2. No references to `.claude/settings.json` or `.codex/config.toml` in install code
3. No references to `SessionStart` in production code
4. `install.sh` no longer creates `.vendored/hooks/` or downloads vendored-session.sh
5. Core `git-vendored install <tool>@<version>` still works (no regression to host-side flow)

title: Remove hook test suite and update remaining tests
body:
Delete tests that exercise the dead session-hook code paths. Do not skip — delete entirely.

### Delete:
- `tests/test_setup_hooks.py` (entire file, ~213 lines) — tests for setup_hooks_claude, setup_hooks_codex, TestOrchestratorTemplate, etc.

### Audit and update:
- `tests/test_install.py` (~3700 lines) — remove any test methods that call setup_hooks, list_hooks, or reference --setup-hooks flag
- `tests/test_installer.py` (~250 lines) — remove any tests for bootstrap hook setup (vendored-session.sh download, .vendored/hooks/ creation)

### Keep:
- All tests for core vendoring (install, check, audit, manifests)
- Tests for run_post_install_hook() (still used)
- Tests for list_vendors() (still used)

## Acceptance criteria

1. `tests/test_setup_hooks.py` no longer exists
2. No test references to setup_hooks, list_hooks, vendored-session.sh, or SessionStart
3. Full test suite passes (`python -m pytest`)
4. No tests are skipped as a substitute for deletion

title: Close obsolete tooling-fixes P0 (shim resolution) and P5 (hook failure handling)
body:
Two pre-existing tooling-fixes issues are now moot after the image-baked migration:

- **P0 — dynamic shim resolution**: no shims at session time anymore because there is no .vendored/pkg/ to point at.
- **P5 — hook failure handling**: no hooks run at session time anymore.

### Action:
1. Search for these issues in pearls (`prl list`) and any other tracking (GitHub issues, docs/planning/)
2. If they exist as prl issues: close them as obsolete with a note pointing to gv-e584
3. If they exist only in planning docs: add a note marking them obsolete and referencing this epic
4. If they don't exist as tracked issues: document in the gv-e584 epic that they were mentioned in planning but never formally tracked, and are now moot

Also search for any other open issues under epics gv-3adc (session hooks) and gv-7a53 (codex/DX) that reference hook infrastructure — those may also be obsolete.

## Acceptance criteria

1. P0 and P5 are either closed or documented as moot
2. Any other hook-dependent issues identified and flagged

title: Update documentation to reflect narrowed scope
body:
Update all documentation to reflect that session-hook orchestration has been intentionally removed. Readers should know it was deleted, not forgotten.

### Files to update:
- `README.md` — lines ~129, ~215-216 mention hooks directory. Update to describe the narrowed scope (host-side git-semver/git-dogfood installs only). Add a "Removed" or "History" note about session-hook orchestration.
- `docs/adoption.md` — lines ~112-150 (Codex Compatibility section) reference session hooks, vendored-session.sh, SessionStart. Update or remove these sections.
- `docs/vendor-install-dir-guide.md` — check for hook references and update.

### Workflow files:
- Audit `.github/workflows/` for any hook-specific steps or references. The workflows that run `.vendored/check` or `.vendored/audit` for core vendoring should stay; only hook-specific bits go.

### Do NOT create:
- AGENTS.md (does not exist in this repo currently — don't create one unless the scope changes)

## Acceptance criteria

1. README explicitly states session-hook orchestration was removed and why
2. docs/adoption.md no longer references vendored-session.sh or SessionStart as current functionality
3. No documentation references hooks as if they still exist
4. Workflow files contain no hook-specific steps

title: Regression verify: host-side git-semver and git-dogfood installs
body:
Non-negotiable regression check. The entire point of this epic is "remove dead session-hook stuff WITHOUT breaking surviving host-side installs."

### Verify end-to-end:
1. In a clean environment, run `git-vendored install git-semver@<latest>` — confirm `git semver` works
2. In a clean environment, run `git-vendored install git-dogfood@<latest>` — confirm `git dogfood` works
3. Run `.vendored/check` — confirm it passes for both tools
4. Run `.vendored/audit` — confirm it passes

### What "works" means:
- The tool binary/script is placed at the correct path
- The manifest files (.version, .post-installed) are written correctly
- The tool executes successfully (e.g., `git semver --help` returns 0)

### If either breaks:
- Something was over-deleted in .1/.2/.3 — identify what and fix before closing the epic

## Acceptance criteria

1. `git-vendored install git-semver@<version>` produces a working `git semver` command
2. `git-vendored install git-dogfood@<version>` produces a working `git dogfood` command
3. `.vendored/check` and `.vendored/audit` pass
4. This issue is the LAST one closed in the epic — it gates the epic closure
